explicit_text: Shaun Maloney was a controversial figure in the union world. He was expelled from both the Teamsters and the Sailors' Union of the Pacific, possibly the only person to be expelled for life from both. Despite this, he was involved in union disputes in 1949, and his records and correspondence with my dad are kept at the University of Washington. He never got back into the Sailors' Union, unlike my dad who was gradually reinstated. Shaun was connected with John Mahoney and Dick Moork through the Socialist Party, though less actively.

explicit_text: The Korean War affected union work and fishing schedules. During wartime, if business on the waterfront was slow, fishermen like my dad could go fishing, but if the war was active, they were often not allowed to go. The union had rules about this, and sometimes money earned from fishing had to be paid back to the union. This wartime context shaped much of the maritime labor environment in the early 1950s.
